title: Get help: Family violence helplines and services - Canada.ca
content:
## Get help: Family violence helplines and services
If you’re in  immediate danger or need urgent medical support, call 9-1-1.
Find resources,  helplines and services in your area.
- National       services and resources related to family violence
- Helplines       and services in your province or territory

## National services and resources  related to family violence
Sheltersafe.ca : Information and a clickable map to help connect women and their children  across Canada with the nearest shelter for safety and support.
iHEAL app : A free, private and secure app to help Canadian women who have  experienced abuse from a current or past partner find ways to stay safe and be  well.
Intimate partner violence against men and boys : Information and resources for support,  validation, advice and referrals.

#### For youth and young adults
Kids Help  Phone : 24/7 mental health  support across Canada for kids, teens and young adults. Call 1-800-668-6868 or  text CONNECT to 686868.

#### For Indigenous Peoples
Hope for Wellness Helpline : 24/7 support for Indigenous Peoples across  Canada. Call 1-855-242-3310 (free) or connect online to reach experienced and  culturally competent counsellors. Services available in English and French and,  on request, in Cree, Ojibway and Inuktitut.
Canadian Network for the  Prevention of Elder Abuse (CNPEA) : Information and links to resources for you or someone you know who is  experiencing abuse or neglect as an older adult.
Trans Lifeline : Confidential and  anonymous trans peer support and crisis hotline. Run by and for the transgender  community. Call 1-877-330-6366.
Get help with family violence : Information from the Department of Justice  on the laws relating to family violence and the kind of help that is available.

#### Mental health support
Get mental health support : Services and helplines if you’re in crisis  and need emotional support.

## Helplines and services in your province or  territory
The following is a  listing of helplines and services in each province or territory for people  experiencing any type of family violence.
